name: SPL Crypto Module
description: Real cryptographic implementations for SPL token verification, including SHA-256 hashing, Ed25519 signature verification, Merkle proof verification, HKDF key derivation, and hash chain verification. Ed25519 operations require the optional 'cryptography' package, while hash operations use only stdlib (hashlib, hmac).

functions.name: sha256
functions.description: Computes SHA-256 hash of input data and returns raw digest bytes.

functions.name: sha256_hex
functions.description: Computes SHA-256 hash of input data and returns hex-encoded string.
postconditions: Returns 64-character hex string representing SHA-256 digest

functions.name: verify_ed25519
functions.description: Verifies an Ed25519 signature over a message using a public key. Requires the 'cryptography' package to be installed.

inputs.name: public_key_hex
inputs.description: Ed25519 public key as hex string

error_data.message: Ed25519 verification requires the 'cryptography' package. Install with: pip install agent-safe-spl[crypto]

postconditions: Returns True if signature is valid
Returns False if signature is invalid or hex decoding fails

functions.name: verify_merkle_proof
functions.description: Verifies a Merkle proof for leaf_data against an expected root hash. Leaf data is UTF-8 encoded and hashed, then combined with proof steps to reconstruct the root.

inputs.name: leaf_data
inputs.description: Leaf value that will be SHA-256 hashed

inputs.name: proof
inputs.description: List of proof steps with 'hash' (hex) and 'position' ('left' or 'right') keys

inputs.name: root_hex
inputs.description: Expected Merkle root hash as hex string

postconditions: Returns True if computed root matches root_hex
Returns False on any exception (invalid hex, missing keys, etc.)

functions.name: hash_tuple
functions.description: Hashes a list of values by JSON-serializing them (compact format) and then computing SHA-256 hex digest.

inputs.name: values
inputs.description: List of values to hash (must be JSON-serializable)

postconditions: Returns hex-encoded SHA-256 hash of compact JSON representation

functions.name: _hkdf_sha256
functions.description: HKDF-SHA256 (RFC 5869) extract-and-expand key derivation function. Uses only stdlib (no external dependencies).

inputs.name: salt
inputs.description: Salt value (defaults to 32 zero bytes if empty)

inputs.name: length
inputs.description: Desired output key length in bytes

postconditions: Returns derived key material of exactly 'length' bytes
Uses 32 zero bytes as salt if input salt is empty

functions.name: derive_service_key
functions.description: Derives a service-specific Ed25519 keypair using HKDF-SHA256 for unlinkability. Different services see different public keys derived from the same master key. Requires the 'cryptography' package.

inputs.name: master_key_hex
inputs.description: 32-byte master private key seed as hex string

preconditions: master_key_hex must be valid hex encoding 32 bytes
postconditions: Derives 32-byte seed using HKDF with service_domain as info parameter

functions.name: verify_hash_chain
functions.description: Verifies a hash chain receipt by hashing the preimage (chain_length - index) times and comparing to the commitment.

inputs.name: commitment
inputs.description: Expected final hash value as hex string

inputs.name: preimage_hex
inputs.description: Starting preimage value as hex string

inputs.name: index
inputs.description: Current index in the hash chain

inputs.name: chain_length
inputs.description: Total length of the hash chain

postconditions: Returns True if hashing preimage (chain_length - index) times equals commitment
Returns False on any exception (invalid hex, etc.)

invariants: HKDF salt defaults to 32 zero bytes when empty or falsy
HKDF uses fixed salt 'agent-safe-v1' for service key derivation

data_access.rationale: Pure cryptographic functions with no persistent data access

authority.rationale: Module provides cryptographic primitives for hashing, signature verification, key derivation, and Merkle/hash-chain verification
